title: Nuclei isolation from human kidney for single-nucleus RNA-seq

steps: Precool all instruments and buffers. All steps are performed on ice or in cold room (4°C) to minimize RNA degradation.
Start with kidney cubes (either fresh or snap frozen, smaller than 8 mm 3 ) and place on a 60 mm dish.
Add 1 ml of NLB1, then mince very well with a fresh razor blade. undetermined, NLB1
Transfer the minced tissue with NLB1 into a Dounce tissue grinder and add another 1 ml of NLB1. undetermined, NLB1

Grind 20-30 times with a loose pestle, then pass the homogenate through a 200 µm strainer and collect in a 50 ml conical tube. To reduce heat caused by friction, homogenization should be performed gently on ice.
Transfer the homogenate again into a Dounce tissue grinder.
Grind the homogenate 10-15 times with a tight pestle.
Add 2 ml of NLB1 and incubate for 5 min on ice. undetermined, NLB1 00:05:00, Incubation on ice
Pass the homogenate through a 40 µm strainer into 50 ml conical tube.
Transfer the homogenate into a 15 ml conical tube.
Centrifuge the tube at 500 G for 5 min at 4°C. Undetermined, Centrifugation 00:05:00, Centrifugation at 500 G
Carefully suspend the pellet in 4 ml of NLB2. undetermined, NLB2
Incubate on ice for 5 min. 00:05:00, Incubation on ice
Centrifuge at 500 G for 5 min at 4°C. We use a fixed angle rotor. Undetermined, Centrifugation 00:05:00, Centrifugation at 500 G
Carefully suspend the pellet in 2 ml of NSB. undetermined, NSB
Pass the suspension through a 5 µm strainer into 50 ml conical tube.
creator: Ben Humphreys
